front: Photograph: shows Black janitor Ella Watson holding a broom and mop in front of an American flag, echoing Grant Wood's American Gothic to comment on racial inequality; taken by the photographer on his first day at the FSA
Artist: first Black/African-American staff photographer for Life magazine
Film: 1971 blaxploitation film directed by Gordon Parks; Isaac Hayes wrote its Oscar-winning funk theme
Photograph: shows a man emerging from a manhole holding a manhole cover over his head; part of a collaboration with Ralph Ellison for two largely unpublished essays
back: The Invisible Man (Gordon Parks and Ralph Ellison)
work: The Invisible Man (photo-essay with Ralph Ellison)

front: Photograph: Gordon Parks earned his Life magazine position with this series documenting the daily life of a Harlem gang leader
Artist: worked for the Farm Security Administration after buying a camera from a pawnshop and teaching himself photography
Artist: documented Jim Crow Alabama in the Life essay 'The Restraints: Open and Hidden'; the Causey family was forced to leave town after its publication
Artist: directed The Learning Tree, adapted from his own coming-of-age novel; his son Gordon Parks Jr. directed Super Fly before dying in a 1979 plane crash
Artist: composed a Symphonic Set for Piano and Orchestra, premiered in Venice with conductor Dean Dixon
Artist: his photographs of nuns and rural Alabama children were recreated in the music video for Kendrick Lamar's 'ELEMENT'
